subject: History
sub_strand: Why and How We Study History
content_standard: B1.1.1. Demonstrate understanding of what history is about and how it is part of everyday life.
indicator_description: Explain that history deals with past human activities
examplars:
1. Mention the activity they witnessed on their first day at school
2. Identify any activity performed in the past which has not been performed again
3. List any past events they have witnessed
4. Explain that since those activities were performed in the past, they qualify to be called history
5. Learners sing a song, recite a rhyme or watch a documentary of any past activity.

subject: History
sub_strand: Why and How We Study History
content_standard: B1.1.1. Demonstrate understanding of what history is about and how it is part of everyday life.
indicator_description: Describe how sources of historical evidence help us find out about past human activities
examplars:
1. Sing songs or recite rhymes related to celebrations such as birthdays, festivals and weddings as part of history.
2. Sketch/show a video of a scene depicting a birthday, funeral, wedding and festival celebration
3. Start a birthday partnership/register to make history alive in the classroom. Learners write their date of birth chronologically on a manila card and this should be hanged in the classroom. At the end of every history lesson, the class could refer to these records for an upcoming birthday.
4. Build an album of themselves and family members, starting with earlier pictures and photographs to resent ones

subject: History
content_standard: B1.1.4.2. Recount history about their communities.
indicator_description: State the similarities and differences between the communities where learners live.
examplars:
1. Visit historical sites in town e.g. an old chapel, a post office, a chief's palace, grooves, forests, wetlands etc.
2. Learners list the names of historical sites starting with the oldest to the most recent
3. learners sketch what they have seen on their visit
4. Compare sketches drawn with pictures of historical sites from other communities
5. Learners discuss their sketches bringing out similarities and differences in the sites they visited

subject: History
content_standard: B1.2.3.1. Demonstrate understanding of why Ghana used to be called the 'Gold Coast'
indicator_description: Explain why, in the past, Ghana was known as the Gold Coast.
examplars:
1. Explain how the name was changed from Gold Coast to Ghana Discuss the English translation of the Portuguese name costa da mina - Gold Coast
2. Use the internet to locate ancient Ghana empire and the Akan forest regions of Ghana
3. Discuss the role of Dr J.B. Danquah in linking the civilisations of the ancient Ghana Empire to the Akan of the forest region of Ghana

subject: History
content_standard: B1.2.3.1. Demonstrate understanding of why Ghana used to be called the 'Gold Coast'
indicator_description: Recall when the name Ghana came into formal use.
examplars:
1. Discuss the sequence of events leading to the change of name
2. Discuss the role of played by parliament and other individuals in the change of name from Gold Coast to Ghana
3. Dramatise a scene in parliament in changing the name Gold Coast to Ghana
4. Explain how similarly a new independent country can take on a new name, a new flag, a new leader in a story form. (A resource person could be invited)

subject: History
content_standard: B1.2.5.1. Show understanding of significant roles played by Ghanaians from different walks of life
indicator_description: Identify Ghanaians of diverse fields who have contributed significantly to national development including Theodosia Okoh National flag. Amon Kotei - Coat of Arms. Baba Yara - Football for Ghana. Ephraim Amu - Music, Kofi Antubam - Art and craft, Kow Ansah - Film etc.
examplars:
1. Identify Ghanaians of diverse professions who have contributed significantly to national development
2. Match individuals with their achievements e. g Theodosia Okoh - national flag.
3. Amon Kotei - coat of arms. Baba Yara - football for Ghana. Ephraim Amu- music, Kofi Antubam- art and craft, Kow Ansah - Film etc.
4. Show and discuss a documentary of significant individuals
5. Guide learners to study about these individuals using the internet
6. Role play what learners want to be in future

subject: History
content_standard: B1.3.1.1 Show understanding of Europeans who came to Ghana
indicator_description: Explore which Europeans came to Ghana
examplars:
1. Identify the European countries whose citizens came and settled in Ghana Portugal, Britain, France, Sweden, Germany, (Brandenburg) Denmark, Norway, Netherlands
2. Arrange the Europeans which they came to Ghana in the order which they came (starting with those who came first)
3. Locate each of these countries on the world map using the internet.
4. Find out where each country first settled in Ghana (From parents, museums etc.)

subject: History
content_standard: B1.6.1.1. Demonstrate understanding of the presidents that have ruled Ghana since 1960
indicator_description: Identify the Presidents Ghana has had since 1960
examplars:
1. Name the presidents of Ghana since 1960.
2. Match pictures of Ghana's presidents with their names (Create an album with the pictures).
3. Match the names of presidents of Ghana with their dates of tenure (Add the names to their pictures).
4. Put learners in groups to create a gallery station: Select a picture of one of the presidents; Display the picture on a large cardboard; Write the name below; Add length of tenure.
5. Invite learners to visit the gallery station and talk about each group's presentation

subject: History
content_standard: B2.2.1.1. Demonstrate knowledge of the ethnic groups in Ghana
indicator_description: Identify the ethnic groups in each region in Ghana
examplars:
1. Identify the administrative regions of Ghana
2. Locate the ethnic groups in each region on a map of Ghana
3. With the aid of a slide/video/TV/internet, identify the regions and ethnic groups in Ghana
4. Match the ethnic groups with their region
5. List the administrative regions in the order in which they created (starting with the oldest region)

subject: History
content_standard: B2.2.1.1. Show understanding of the characteristics of some of the ethnic groups in Ghana
indicator_description: State the characteristics of the ethnic groups in Ghana
examplars:
1. Identify the characteristics (food, festivals, dressing, language, political institutions, social and economic activities, taboos, oaths etc.) of some of the ethnic groups in Ghana
2. Show documentary/pictures/ internet of a festival of one of the ethnic groups
3. Discuss what the documentary entails.

subject: History
content_standard: B2.2.4.1. Exhibit knowledge of the history of the major historical locations in Ghana
indicator_description: Discuss the history of Ghana's major historical locations.
examplars:
1. Identify the major historical locations in Ghana (starting with the oldest location).
2. Show and discuss video/documentary/stories/slides about the history of these major historical locations in Ghana.
3. Retell the history of these major historical locations in Ghana.
4. Interact with knowledgeable persons/ opinion leaders/ chiefs/ resource persons to tell the history of any historical location in your community or district.
5. Visit any historical site in their community.
6. Retell the history of major sites in their community.

subject: History
content_standard: B2.2.5.1.1 Exhibit understanding of Ghanaian women who have contributed significantly to Ghana's development
indicator_description: Identify Ghanaian women who have made significant contributions to national development
examplars:
1. Name some outstanding women in the history of Ghana
2. Match these women with their achievements in Ghana (e.g. Mrs Charity Zormelo-Fiawoo, Squadron Leader Melody Danquah, Elizabeth Ohene, Justice Georgina Wood, Justice Joyce Bamford-Addo)
3. Retell the contributions of these leaders using anecdotes/videos
4. Think pair-share-activity: Individual learners identify a woman who they admire most in the community and discuss reasons.

subject: History
content_standard: B2.3.2.1. Understand that trade was the major reason why the Europeans settled in Ghana
indicator_description: Describe how early trade was carried out between Ghanaians and Europeans
examplars:
1. Describe how early trade was carried out between Ghanaians and the Europeans
2. Organise learning corners with real items or pictures (Gin, Gun, Textiles, Tobacco, Ivory, Gold)
3. Role play the barter trade in those items.
4. Use internet to identify countries with items that remained part of Ghanaian life (e.g. Dutch wax prints).
5. Name the towns that emerged on the coast as a result of European trade.

subject: History
content_standard: B3.2.1.1. Exhibit knowledge of the unique history of the major ethnic groups in Ghana
indicator_description: Discuss the origins of the major ethnic groups in Ghana
examplars:
1. Identify some of the major ethnic groups. Eg. Guan, Mole-Dagbani/ Gonja, Akan, Ga/Adangme, Ewe
2. Use a map to trace the routes the major ethnic groups travelled to get to their present-day locations in Ghana.
3. In groups, learners discuss the origin of the major ethnic groups
4. Compose a song with names of the major ethnic groups and where they originated
5. Draw a map to locate the migratory routes of the ethnic groups.
6. Develop a timeline of the periods each ethnic group arrived in Ghana

subject: History
content_standard: B3 2.2.1. Demonstrate knowledge of how intergroup alliances, conflicts and other exchanges
indicator_description: Discuss the nature of exchanges among the ethnic groups.
examplars:
1. Identify the forms of exchanges including forms of trade, inter-marriage, conflict and alliances.
2. Discuss the benefit of these exchanges

subject: History
content_standard: B3 2.2.1. Demonstrate knowledge of how intergroup alliances, conflicts and other exchanges
indicator_description: Name some of the items exchanged among the various groups.
examplars:
1. Identify trade items: salt, fish, gold, kola, Shea butter, pottery, cloth, iron utensils
2. Match items with the areas/sources where items were obtained
3. Explain the medium of exchange
4. Trace the origin of the name 'cedi'

subject: History
content_standard: B3 2.2.1. Demonstrate knowledge of how intergroup alliances, conflicts and other exchanges
indicator_description: Describe the conflicts and alliances that existed among the ethnic groups in Ghana
examplars:
1. Discuss intergroup wars such as Battle of Feyiase and the Krepi war of 1833.
2. Discuss instances where alliances were formed to fight a common enemy (e.g. Battle of Akatamanso).

subject: History
content_standard: B3.2.4.1. Demonstrate knowledge of the forts and castle built in Ghana.
indicator_description: Identify the forts and castles built along the coast of Ghana
examplars:
1. Explain the differences between forts and castles
2. Use internet to identify major forts and castles on a map (location, builders, when and why)
3. Explain uses since built (seat of government, trading, education)
4. Discuss uses since independence
5. Match forts and castles with locations
6. Draw favorite castle
7. Show and discuss documentary/slides
8. Visit and model a fort or castle

subject: History
content_standard: B3.2.5.1. Describe the contributions of some outstanding Ghanaian entrepreneurs.
indicator_description: Describe Ghanaian entrepreneurs who have made significant contributions
examplars:
1. Name Ghanaian entrepreneurs of note (e.g. George Grant, Winifred Tete-Ansa, Esther Ocloo, B.A. Mensah, Kwabena Pepra, J.K. Siaw, Alhaji Adam Iddrisu)
2. Show pictures of outstanding entrepreneurs
3. Retell life stories from a documentary
4. Identify entrepreneurs whose work inspires and give reasons

subject: History
content_standard: B3.3.1.1. Show understanding of the interactions between the local people and early settlers
indicator_description: Describe how the Europeans settled on the Gold Coast, including forming alliances with the local chiefs
examplars:
1. Dramatise the meeting between the Portuguese and Kwamina Ansah
2. Explain why other Europeans who came later also settled on the Gold Coast
3. Investigate settlement methods including alliances and marrying local people

subject: History
sub_strand: Why and How We Study HISTORY
content_standard: B4.1.1.1. Show understanding of the importance of studying history
indicator_description: Explain the importance of studying the history of Ghana.
examplars:
1. Engage learners to discuss how history defines our identity as Ghanaians
2. Role-play to show how history teaches us customs, values, and traditions
3. Role play how history can promote national pride, patriotism and integration

subject: History
sub_strand: Why and How We Study HISTORY
content_standard: B4.1.1.2. Understand the sources for writing history
indicator_description: Identify the sources of history including archaeology, numismatics, oral tradition, wall paintings etc.
examplars:
1. List sources: funeral brochures, oral tradition, old photos, letters, diaries, arts/crafts, old newspapers, dirges, appellation and books.
2. Classify sources under primary and secondary.
3. Explain relevance of information from old newspapers and diaries (e.g. natural disasters).

subject: History
content_standard: B4.2.1.1. Show understanding some of the factors that led to the rise, expansion and decline of one major kingdom
indicator_description: Describe how one major Kingdom was formed and the reasons behind its expansion.
examplars:
1. Identify reasons for rise, expansion and decline of one Kingdom (Savanna, Forest, or Coastal Belt states)
2. Using map of Ghana, locate the kingdom
3. Play games/animations on location of major Kingdoms.

subject: History
content_standard: B4.2.1.1. Show understanding some of the factors that led to the rise, expansion and decline of one major kingdom
indicator_description: State the factors that led to decline of the Kingdom you have studied.
examplars: 1. Give reasons why kingdoms declined: internal disputes (rebellions, succession), British interference, conquest, desire to expand territories.

subject: History
content_standard: B4.1.4.1. Demonstrate knowledge of the history of some historical locations in Ghana
indicator_description: Describe the history of Ghana's major historical locations, specifically, Flagstaff house, Burma camp, James Town light house, Gbewa Palace, Larabanga Mosque etc.
examplars:
1. Identify major historical locations in Ghana using internet (location, builder, when, why)
2. Explain uses since built
3. Match locations to map
4. Show and discuss documentary/slides
5. Retell stories behind locations (Flagstaff house, GBC, Burma camp, etc.)

subject: History
content_standard: B4.2.5.1. Demonstrate understanding of the role of significant traditional rulers in Ghana's history
indicator_description: Identify the role played by some traditional rulers in the national development
examplars:
1. Name traditional chiefs and queen-mothers (e.g. Dode Akaibi, Ndewura Jakpa, Osei Tutu I, Gbewaa, Togbe Sri I, Sir Ofori Atta, Nana Kobina Nketsia I) and state significance
2. Show photographs/documentaries on their roles
3. Retell life story from documentary
4. Identify rulers whose work inspires and give reasons

subject: History
content_standard: B4.3.3.1. Demonstrate knowledge of Europeans' engagement in other activities other than trade in Ghana
indicator_description: Describe European missionary activities in Ghana
examplars:
1. Identify missionary societies that came to Ghana in chronological order
2. Locate places where they operated (e.g. Basel-Akuapem, Bremen-Peki)
3. Discuss major impacts: Christianity and formal education
4. Show and discuss video/documentary of activities
5. Discuss other activities (trade, vocational training, health, alphabets, translation)
6. Debate effects of European activities

subject: History
strand: Colonisation and Developments under Colonial Rule in Ghana
content_standard: B4.4.1.1. Demonstrate understanding of the gradual process of colonisation and recall the initial steps the British took to colonise Ghana.
indicator_description: Examine the Bond of 1844
examplars:
1. Discuss events leading to the signing of the Bond of 1844.
2. Provide content of 1844 [1884 typo in source] using primary evidence and discuss.
3. Show Palaver Hall using internet/pictures.
4. Brainstorm the significance of the Bond.

subject: History
strand: Colonisation and Developments under Colonial Rule in Ghana
content_standard: B4.4.1.2. Show understanding of how the various parts of the country were brought together.
indicator_description: Describe how the different areas The Colony, Asante, The Northern Territories and The British Mandated Togoland-became one territory known as the Gold Coast
examplars:
1. Define the term colonialism
2. Role play the role of George Ekem Ferguson in the acquisition of Northern Territories.
3. Illustrate how Asante and Northern Territories became protectorates using map
4. Locate British Togoland on map
5. Compare 1955 Gold Coast map with modern Ghana map and discuss

subject: History
content_standard: B4.6.1.1. Understand that there have been four Republics in Ghana
indicator_description: Explain that Ghana up to June 1960, though independent, had the Queen of The United Kingdom (UK) as Head of State
examplars:
1. Define the term Republic
2. Discuss limitations on Ghana's independence up to June 1960
3. Use internet to identify positions controlled by British officials up to 1960

subject: History
content_standard: B4.6.1.1. Understand that there have been four Republics in Ghana
indicator_description: State the dates and names of the leaders of the four Republics of Ghana since 1960
examplars:
1. Differentiate between 'President' and 'Head of State'
2. Match the names of the leaders of the four Republics to the period in which they ruled

subject: History
content_standard: B5.2.1.1. Demonstrate understanding of how life in ancient time was different from life today
indicator_description: Describe how our ancestors lived in ancient times (before the 15th century) and compare it with how we live today.
examplars:
1. Identify food, clothes, and travel of ancestors.
2. Compare life today to ancient days (Food, Clothes, Mode of travel, Buildings, Communication, Trading, Professions, Technology).
3. Produce a photo album of family members.
4. Visit ancient sites and museums.
5. Use videos/documentaries/internet to highlight changes.

subject: History
content_standard: B5.2.1.1. Demonstrate understanding of how life in ancient time was different from life today
indicator_description: Describe some ancient towns in Ghana.
examplars:
1. List ancient towns (Begho, Bono-Manso, Dawhenya, Eguafo, Kintampo, Salaga, Daboya)
2. Locate towns on a map of Ghana
3. Use internet to learn about these places and share
4. Show and discuss documentary/pictures

subject: History
content_standard: B5.2.5.1. Demonstrate knowledge of Ghanaians who made significant contributions locally and internationally
indicator_description: Name Ghanaians who have made significant Contribution locally and internationally
examplars:
1. Identify individuals using pictures, posters or internet (e.g. Dr R.E.G Armattoe, Kofi Annan, Osibisa, El Anatsui, David Adjaye, Efua Sutherland, etc.)
2. Match personalities with areas of specialty
3. Show photographs/documentary of significant individuals
4. Retell live stories from documentary
5. Develop a documentary/poster of individuals
6. Identify rulers/individuals whose work inspires and give reasons

subject: History
sub_strand: International Trade Including the Slave Trade
content_standard: B5.3.2.1. Understand that what began as trade in goods from 1471 soon included trade in humans by the 16th century.
indicator_description: Investigate why the Europeans began trading in humans by the 16th century.
examplars:
1. Use map to explain Trans-Atlantic slave trade concept
2. Role play/show documentary on how trade was conducted
3. Brainstorm reasons for trading in humans
4. Visit/show pictures of forts and castles
5. Develop a poster on the Trans-Atlantic Slave Trade in small groups
6. Groups present work to class

subject: History
strand: Colonisation and Developments under Colonial Rule in Ghana
content_standard: B5.4.2.1. Demonstrate understanding of the social developments that took place during the colonial period (1874-1957).
indicator_description: Identify the developments in education during the colonial era (1874-1957).
examplars:
1. Identify schools existing by 1930 and their locations
2. List measures taken by British to promote education (e.g. Ordinances for teacher registration)
3. Discuss role of churches in education sector
4. Recall role of important people (e.g. Dr Kwegyir Aggrey) with pictures

subject: History
strand: Colonisation and Developments under Colonial Rule in Ghana
content_standard: B5.4.2.1. Demonstrate understanding of the social developments that took place during the colonial period (1874-1957).
indicator_description: Identify some of the health facilities and housing projects in the colonial period.
examplars:
1. Discuss health facilities and housing projects (e.g. Korle Bu hospital 1923, Kumasi, Takoradi hospitals)
2. Brainstorm significance of these services
3. Show pictures/documentaries to highlight facilities

subject: History
strand: Colonisation and Developments under Colonial Rule in Ghana
content_standard: B5.4.3.2. Show understanding of the Economic policies and projects during the colonial period (1874-1957)
indicator_description: Describe the economic measures introduced during the colonial period including transport and communication projects.
examplars:
1. Identify economic policies and projects during colonial era
2. Enumerate contributions in agriculture (e.g. Bunso station, Aburi botanical garden, Kwadaso College, cocoa promotion)
3. Discuss construction of Takoradi Harbour, roads, railways, and vehicles
4. Discuss Tetteh Quarshie's role in cocoa
5. Visit/show pictures of facilities (e.g. Tetteh Quarshie farm)
6. Enumerate contributions to mining sector

subject: History
content_standard: B5.5.1.1. Show understanding of the factors that led to the formation of early protest movements in Ghana before 1945
indicator_description: Identify the early protest movements in Ghana before 1945.
examplars:
1. Define protest movement
2. List movements: ARPS (1897), NCBWA (1917), Gold Coast Youth Conference (1929)
3. Discuss sequence of events leading to formation

subject: History
content_standard: B5.5.1.1. Show understanding of the factors that led to the formation of early protest movements in Ghana before 1945
indicator_description: Examine sources of evidence about the role of Joseph Mensah Sarbah in the Aborigines Rights Protection Society-ARPS-1897
examplars:
1. Identify key leaders of ARPS by pictures/documentary
2. Find out from internet how ARPS got land bill withdrawn
3. Present report as a poster

subject: History
content_standard: B5.5.1.1. Show understanding of the factors that led to the formation of early protest movements in Ghana before 1945
indicator_description: Examine sources of evidence about the role of Joseph Ephraim Casely-Hayford in the National Congress of British West Africa (NCBWA).
examplars:
1. Identify Casely-Hayford from photographs
2. Show and discuss documentary of his bust at Casford Hall
3. Present narrative of his leadership role in NCBWA
4. Learners retell the story

subject: History
content_standard: B5.5.1.1. Show understanding of the factors that led to the formation of early protest movements in Ghana before 1945
indicator_description: Examine sources of evidence about the role of Dr Joseph Boakye Danquah in the Gold Coast Youth Conference
examplars:
1. Present narrative of J.B. Danquah's role in the conference
2. Field trip to Danquah Circle or his home (or show picture)
3. Learners retell the story of J.B. Danquah and the Conference

subject: History
content_standard: B5.5.3.1. Show understanding of the sequence of events leading to the 1948 riots in Ghana
indicator_description: Explain why people were unhappy in the country after the Second World War
examplars:
1. Show/discuss documentary on state of affairs (failure to honor promises to soldiers, housing lack, high costs)
2. Discuss government handling of grievances
3. Role play the scene and offer opinions
4. Develop poster linking boycott, shootings, and riots

subject: History
content_standard: B5.5.3.1. Show understanding of the sequence of events leading to the 1948 riots in Ghana
indicator_description: Examine sources of evidence about what happened during the 1948 riots.
examplars:
1. Show map of Accra indicating approved routes for ex-service men
2. Use documentary to illustrate riots and discuss
3. Role play scene of 1948 riots
4. Discuss arrest of 'Big Six' (leaders of UGCC)

subject: History
content_standard: B6.3.4.1. Demonstrate knowledge of the impact of European presence on Ghana
indicator_description: Assess the changes that the European presence brought to Ghana.
examplars:
1. Show and discuss documentary of European presence
2. Discuss activities (trade, vocational training, health, alphabets, translation)
3. Debate the impacts of European presence

subject: History
strand: Colonisation and Developments under Colonial Rule in Ghana
content_standard: B6 3.4.1. Show understanding of Political developments under colonial rule, 1874-1957.
indicator_description: Describe the features of British colonial rule in Ghana including 'direct' and 'indirect' rule, 1874-1957.
examplars:
1. Explain 'direct' and 'indirect' rule concepts
2. Discuss features of both systems
3. Discuss division of colony into provinces
4. Discuss advantages and disadvantages of systems
5. Role play indirect rule

subject: History
content_standard: B6.5.2.1. Appreciate the role played by the leadership of political parties in the struggle for independence from 1947 to 1957
indicator_description: Describe the role played by the leaders of the two major political parties (UGCC and CPP) in the independence struggle
examplars:
1. Define a political party
2. Discuss formation of UGCC (1947)
3. Discuss formation of CPP (1949)
4. Discuss roles of leaders of UGCC and CPP
5. Map out specific roles of 'Big Six' members
6. Explain how they became known as 'the Big Six'
7. Show documentary on independence eve rally
8. Recount an Independence Day celebration
9. Enact declaration of independence by Kwame Nkrumah

subject: History
content_standard: B6.5.4.1. Demonstrate an understanding of the course of events leading to independence from 1948 to 1957
indicator_description: Explain post World War II developments in the Gold Coast
examplars:
1. Discuss establishment of Watson Commission
2. Report findings via paper/performance/poster/role play
3. Recount lessons (e.g. recommendation for Coussey Committee)
4. Discuss main recommendations in Coussey report

subject: History
content_standard: B6.5.4.1. Demonstrate an understanding of the course of events leading to independence from 1948 to 1957
indicator_description: Explain how Ghana gained independence through constitutional means.
examplars:
1. Discuss 1951 general elections
2. Use resource person to discuss sequence leading to 1956 elections
3. Discuss outcome of 1956 elections
4. Enact an election polling station scene

subject: History
content_standard: B6.6.1.1. Demonstrate understanding of the Fourth Republic (1992 to date).
indicator_description: Describe the events leading to the emergence of the Fourth Republic.
examplars:
1. Describe process (new constitution, referendum, ban lifting, 1992 elections)
2. List names/tenure of Presidents of Fourth Republic
3. Use internet for biographical details of Presidents
4. Show/discuss documentary on inauguration
5. Recount a witnessed inauguration
6. Discuss other key figures (Chief Justices, Speakers, etc.)

subject: History
content_standard: B6.6.1.1. Demonstrate understanding of the Fourth Republic (1992 to date).
indicator_description: Identify the political parties that have governed the country under the Fourth Republic
examplars:
1. Identify governments that handed over power peacefully
2. List parties and dates of tenure
3. Use internet/resource persons/visits to identify district achievements
4. Discuss findings in class

subject: History
content_standard: 1. Understand that military takeovers interrupted constitutional rule on four occasions since 1966, with some consequences.
indicator_description: Identify the leaders of the coup d'états and names of their regimes.
examplars:
1. List military regimes since 1966 and leaders
2. Show and discuss documentary on takeovers

subject: History
content_standard: 1. Understand that military takeovers interrupted constitutional rule on four occasions since 1966, with some consequences.
indicator_description: Assess the consequences of military takeovers on Ghana's development
examplars:
1. Show and discuss documentary of takeover
2. Brainstorm effects (human rights abuse, overthrow of constitutions, harsh rules)
3. Debate effects of military takeovers
